description: Lighting a lamp at the same point in a remembrance may be a 'thelumo' (ritual). A ritual is a patterned action whose form carries personal or shared significance. It may stand alone or form part of a 'thorea' (ceremony); repeated work intended to develop capacity is 'thole' (practice).
articulatory_notes: Rest the tongue tip at the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th' and let the breath open into middle-height front 'e'. Touch the alveolar ridge for voiced lateral 'l' and round into stressed high-back 'u'. Close the lips for nasal 'm' and finish with middle-height back rounded 'o'.
usage_notes: A person or group may practise a ritual, and nearby language can say what it marks, how often it returns, and who participates. A tradition's own name, wording, and ritual roles accompany the Phi account when they establish identity.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A repeated form can bring attention back to an ordinary act. Repetition alone makes no act wise or sacred; its significance depends on what participants do and understand it to mean.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Ritual can carry memory through gesture, place, timing, and participation when no written instruction would preserve the whole event. 'thelumo' leaves those particulars available rather than replacing them.

semantic_domains.activity: a patterned action performed for personal or shared significance
semantic_domains.community: an observance recognised or maintained among participants
semantic_domains.ritual: meaning carried through an enacted and often repeated form
semantic_domains.temporal: an action whose return or placement in an occasion matters
